theory.canonical_statement: cuum fluctuations slows down just enough \u2013 say its phase velocity drops to\r ~84% of c (since ) \u2013 it might trigger a phase transition to a particle\r with rest mass. if intense\r vacuum correlations gave an index , light would slow to , perhaps precipitating matter formation. The same factor shows up in a Kuramoto model\r analogy for synchronization: if the coupling strength between oscillators is about 18.5%\r above the minimum needed to overcome their frequency difference ( ), phase-locking becomes robust. In other words, nature\r might prefer a safety margin of ~20% for long-term stability in phase synchronization.\r Similarly, in orbital mechanics one might imagine a tidal locking efficacy needing to\r exceed 1 by ~0.185 to truly loc 2024 , which is within ~1.5% of\r 1.1852.
